note: Curated highlights plus major-outlet coverage from GDELT and Google News (past 12 months).

items.title: Europe's record heatwave: does the continent have a new climate?
items.note: Quoted on Europe's accelerated warming: 'Particularly since 1980, there's just been this enormous spike in temperatures.'

items.title: Beyond Denial: How Oil Execs Shaped a Landmark Climate Study
items.note: Quoted that after decades of delay, holding warming to the old targets has become 'functionally impossible.'

items.title: Why a notably warm start to the year foreshadows what's ahead
items.note: Featured his Climate Dashboard and 2026/2027 global temperature projections.

items.title: Why climate scientists have dropped a worst-case scenario
items.note: Quoted on how RCP8.5 wrongly became known as the 'business as usual' scenario through a game of telephone.

items.title: Prepare for El Niño, UN warns: it could be the strongest in decades
items.note: Quoted that 2027 is very likely to be the world's warmest year on record; the feature used his El Niño forecast.

items.title: Climate Change's Worst-Case Scenario Is Officially Canceled
items.note: On the retirement of the RCP8.5 worst-case scenario, drawing on his work with Glen Peters.

items.title: How to vibe code in science: early adopters share their tips
items.note: Featured for using AI to code new climate-data visualizations, including his 3D tree-ring temperature plots.

items.title: WATCH — Why El Niño could make 2026 the hottest year ever recorded | videoclip | Kids News
AI Scientists Help Human Ones Answer Urgent Climate Questions
items.note: Featured on how AI tools are helping researchers tackle urgent climate questions.

items.title: A dramatic, record-setting El Niño may be brewing, forecasters say
Scientists change how El Niño is labeled to keep up with spike in temperature
items.note: Cited on shifting El Niño/La Niña baselines amid record warmth.

items.title: See how climate change is accelerating
items.note: Quoted: 'There is greater acceptance now that there is a detectable acceleration of warming.'

items.title: Can the clean-energy revolution save us from climate catastrophe?
items.note: Quoted on the clean-energy transition — 'Every unit of renewable energy that is used instead of coal is a good thing' — and that ultimately 'what matters is cumulative emissions.'

items.title: 2025 was Earth's 3rd-warmest year on record
items.note: Cited on 2025 temperatures (~1.44°C above preindustrial) despite La Niña.

items.title: 2025 was another exceptionally hot year
items.note: Cited via his Carbon Brief analysis of the drivers of recent warming.

items.title: This chart shows that progress has actually been made on climate change
items.note: Quoted on bending the emissions curve away from worst-case warming scenarios.

items.title: Marine heatwave over Pacific Ocean could lead to flooding in north-west
Opinion | Turns Out Air Pollution Was Good for Something
Climate change is accelerating, scientists find in 'grim' report
items.note: Quoted as a co-author of the Indicators of Global Climate Change study.

items.title: Takeaways from scientists on the Trump administration's work on climate change
items.note: Quoted criticizing a DOE/EPA report for misusing a metric from his climate-model research.
